content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 8, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 8, 2026, focused on enhancing core runtime capabilities and improving system stability. Key updates include the implementation of advanced logging, memory management features, and a standardized streaming callback architecture to resolve technical debt and improve agent reliability.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

### Core Runtime & Logging Enhancements
*   Introduced comprehensive core enhancements including Odi logging, memory lockdown features, and a startup banner.
*   Added runtime improvements such as embedding cache, caller information, safe replacer, and log gating for prompts and responses.
*   Implemented `DISABLE_MEMORY_CREATION` and `ALLOW_MEMORY_SOURCE_IDS` configurations to provide finer control over agent memory. ([#6562](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6562))

### Streaming Architecture Standardization
*   Consolidated eight disparate `onStreamChunk` definitions into a single canonical `StreamChunkCallback` type alias located in `types/components.ts`.
*   Resolved issues related to TTS garbling caused by inconsistent streaming implementations across the codebase. ([#6690](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6690))

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Inter-Agent Coordination ([#6514](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6514))
*   **Discussion Summary:** The community is actively debating patterns for cross-instance communication and agent-to-agent trust.
*   **Key Points:** 
    *   Participants discussed the "cold-start" problem for new agents and the need for persistent peer relationships.
    *   Proposed solutions include using on-chain credentials (via `eliza-plugin-insumer`) to verify wallet trust profiles and using WTRMRK for append-only on-chain attestation of behavioral history.
    *   There is a identified need for capability exchange, allowing agents to hand off tasks (e.g., research vs. experimentation) directly between instances without human mediation.
